commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Chrome DevTools MCP-backed console/network diagnostics to browser tooling, warms Chrome-MCP tab listing, and fixes existing-session select/dialog parity with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main routes `/console` and `/requests` through Playwright-only helpers, while Chrome-MCP existing-session profiles use a separate transport. I did not execute the live failure path in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair is needed because this review found no narrow patch defect; the PR should continue through normal review and CI.

target.title: feat(browser): route chrome mcp diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

Routes Chrome DevTools MCP console/network diagnostics through OpenClaw browser tooling for existing Chrome-MCP sessions, and fixes follow-up action parity gaps found during localhost live staging.

Changes:

- add console message listing/detail support through Chrome DevTools MCP
- add network request listing/detail support through Chrome DevTools MCP
- warm Chrome-MCP attach before cold `/tabs` listing
- handle existing-session `<select>` actions through evaluated DOM fallback
- allow the existing-session dialog hook path to accept the CLI default `timeoutMs`
- add/extend tests for console/network routing, cold tabs, select fallback, and dialog timeout handling

## Validation

Targeted local test run:

```text
NO_COLOR=1 corepack pnpm exec vitest run \
  extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/tabs.attach-only.test.ts
```

Result: 3 files passed, 46 tests passed.

Additional local evidence was recorded in the harness task `TASK-20260521-006`; live staging was localhost-only using an isolated `agent-chrome` profile.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chrome-MCP-backed existing browser sessions could not reliably expose console/network diagnostics through OpenClaw browser routes, cold `/tabs` could fail before an attach warmup, existing-session `<select>` actions used the wrong fill path, and existing-session dialog handling rejected the CLI default `timeoutMs`.
- **Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw Gateway `2026.4.23` on Linux, installed bundle hotpatched at `~/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/server-context-DS-PO4yR.js`, isolated `agent-chrome` Chrome profile, loopback Gateway on `[::1]:18789`, and localhost fixture `http://127.0.0.1:18778/index.html`. No personal/authenticated Chrome profile was attached.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran real `openclaw browser` CLI flows against the hotpatched Gateway and isolated Chrome profile: status, cold tabs, open localhost fixture, tabs after open, snapshot/screenshot, console, network requests, filtered requests, AI snapshot ref click, evaluate after click, expanded action matrix, then focused select/dialog rerun after the select/dialog fix.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the real setup:

  ```text
  Gateway health: Runtime running on PID 1317742; connectivity probe OK; listening on [::1]:18789.
  Primary live smoke summary: finalOk=true; covered status, cold tabs, open, tabs after open, snapshot, screenshot, console, requests, filtered requests, AI snapshot ref click, and evaluate after click.
  Focused select/dialog rerun summary: finalOk=true.
  Console output included: select-dialog-ready; select-changed beta; dialog-result approved.
  Source targeted validation: 3 files passed, 46 tests passed.
  ```

  Durable evidence bundle: https://github.com/ACSKamloops/openclaw-engineering-harness/pull/63
- **Observed result after fix:** `select beta` produced `choice: beta`; dialog handling produced `dialog:approved` and `stateDialog: approved`; console/network routes returned live MCP-backed results; cold `/tabs` no longer failed before opening a tab in the isolated Chrome profile.
- **What was not tested:** Personal/authenticated Chrome profiles, external websites, generic MCP registration, OpenClaw config mutation, credential/auth changes, worker/ACP/scheduler changes, and merge/release packaging were not tested.
target.author: acskamloops
